page: UpCloud HICPU-16xCPU-24GB is a compute-focused virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 24.0 GB of system memory, which equates to a resource density of 1.5 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 100 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile for this shared-core architecture. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10% at 783.9. However, multi-core scaling is average, with stress-ng multi-core performance reaching 12560.98 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10270.0. Memory bandwidth is stable, measuring 107.53 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 59.69 GB/sec for large block write operations. Large-scale text processing and software compilation with Clang also perform within average ranges.

The primary tradeoff of the HICPU-16xCPU-24GB is its shared CPU allocation and low memory-to-core ratio, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications or workloads requiring sustained, high-performance single-thread execution. For example, text generation on a medium 7B LLM model drops to a poor 5.44 tokens/sec. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-effective option for parallelizable, multi-threaded tasks that do not require dedicated CPU cores or massive memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, software compilation, and basic database operations.
description: UpCloud HICPU-16xCPU-24GB is a compute-focused virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is configured with 24.0 GB of RAM (1.5 GB per core) and a bundled 100 GB local SSD. Benchmark results show a distinct contrast between its poor single-core performance (stress-ng single-core score of 783.9) and its average multi-core capabilities, such as a Geekbench multi-core score of 10270.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 149.12 GB/sec. Due to its shared CPU allocation and low memory-to-core ratio, this server is best suited for cost-sensitive, parallelizable workloads that can leverage multiple threads without requiring dedicated CPU resources, such as static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: UpCloud HICPU-16xCPU-24GB is a virtual server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and a 100 GB SSD. It offers average multi-core performance but limited single-core capabilities for parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud HICPU-16xCPU-24GB features 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and a 100 GB SSD, suitable for multi-threaded, cost-sensitive workloads.
tagline: A shared-core, compute-focused virtual server offering sixteen AMD EPYC vCPUs and bundled SSD storage for parallelized, budget-conscious workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
24.0 GB RAM and 100 GB bundled local SSD storage
Average multi-core performance but limited single-core capacity
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded tasks
